member.name: Mr Luke Charters
member.synopsis: Mr Luke Charters is the Labour MP for York Outer , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Sign Language: Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what support is available to hearing parents of deaf children to learn British Sign Language.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government understands the great importance of British Sign Language (BSL) for bridging barriers to communication between hearing and deaf people and appreciates the vital need for families with deaf children to be able to access BSL courses, as early...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Driving: Republic of Ireland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the compatibility of Irish motor insurance requirements for holders of UK driving licences with the principles of the common travel area.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Motor insurers are responsible for setting the terms and conditions of the policies that they offer, and it is for them to decide the level of risk that they take in issuing any policy to a given applicant. If an applicant wishes to drive a vehicle in the...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Church Commissioners: Land
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the hon. Member for Battersea, representing the Church Commissioners, what consultation the Church Commissioners carried out with tenant farmers and local communities before deciding to market land east of York for sale.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Church Commissioners’ property referred to in the question, from the Hon Member for York Outer, relates to the ongoing marketing for sale of approximately 1,600 acres of land at Scoreby, east of York. The Church Commissioners Property Team informed al...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Church Commissioners

writtenQuestions.heading: Bacteria: Medical Treatments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what recent assessment he has made of the adequacy of the development of Bacteriophage Therapy, and when he expects such treatments to be available for use across the NHS.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the potential of bacteriophage therapy as an emerging treatment for certain bacterial infections, particularly where antimicrobial resistance limits available treatment options. However, while early research and clinical use in i...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Bacteria: Medical Treatments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what recent assessment he has made of the effectiveness of the development of Bacteriophage Therapy; and whether such treatments will be available for use in the NHS.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the potential of bacteriophage therapy as an emerging treatment for certain bacterial infections, particularly where antimicrobial resistance limits available treatment options. However, while early research and clinical use in i...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Rare Diseases: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to improve outcomes for children born with Congenital Anomalies and rare diseases; and what steps his Department is taking to improve support available to young
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to improving the lives of those living with rare diseases under the UK Rare Diseases Framework. We published the fifth annual England action plan in February 2026, where we report on the steps we have taken to advance the prior...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Universal Credit
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what steps his Department is taking to support people migrating to Universal Credit from legacy benefits.
writtenQuestions.answerText: All customers have access to support throughout the process, including the Move to Universal Credit Helpline and independent Help to Claim support provided by Citizens Advice, which offers tailored assistance to help people make and manage their claim. We...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Neurodegenerative Diseases: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to improve outcomes for children with neurodegenerative brain conditions.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to improving outcomes for children with neurodegenerative conditions through a combination of earlier diagnosis, better coordinated care, and support for innovation in treatment.Services for children with rare and complex neuro...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Healthcare Assistants: Training
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the adequacy of current regulatory arrangements for the training of healthcare support workers; and what steps his Department is taking to ensure that all staff recei
writtenQuestions.answerText: Healthcare support workers (HCSWs) are not regulated by a statutory professional body. Where HCSWs are working in health and social care services and the provider is registered with the Care Quality Commission (CQC), the CQC regulates the service provider...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Co-careldopa
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to address shortages of Co-careldopa medication.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has been in contact with suppliers of co-careldopa immediate release and modified release tablets. There is good supply availability in the market for United Kingdom patients.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Private Education: Safety
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, how her Department ensures effective oversight where safeguarding concerns are raised about independent schools.
writtenQuestions.answerText: All private schools are required to comply with the Independent School Standards (ISS), and schools that fail to meet the ISS at inspection are subject to regulatory action. Information about regulatory and enforcement action can be found at: https://www....
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Schools: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of artificial intelligence usage in schools on safeguarding.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department recognises that artificial intelligence (AI) presents both opportunities and risks in education and recognises public concerns around these risks. We are clear that technology is not effective unless it is safe. Our ‘Generative AI in educat...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Schools: Safety
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps she is taking to ensure (a) safeguarding standards and (b) accountability are applied consistently across all types of schools.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This government remains committed to providing a clear and simple safeguarding framework for all schools. This is set out in our statutory safeguarding guidance, which all schools and colleges must have regard to. This guidance includes ‘Keeping Children ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Public Places: Planning
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that the revised National Planning Policy Framework supports the creation of (a) safe, (b) inclusive and (c) accessible public space
writtenQuestions.answerText: Between 16 December 2025 and 10 March 2026, the government consulted a new National Planning Policy Framework (NPPF). The consultation on the revised Framework, which can be found on gov.uk here, included proposals relating to the provision of safe, inclu...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Security Benefits: Medical Examinations
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, whether his Department will consider introducing a supported entry pathway into PIP and Work Capability Assessment roles for newly qualified nurses.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department keeps its approach to recruitment for Personal Independence Payment and Work Capability Assessment health professionals (HPs) under continuous review, to ensure it supports high-quality, safe and consistent assessments for claimants. At pre...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Prescriptions: Fees and Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether there are plans to review the NHS medical exemption criteria for prescription charges, and assess the merits of adding asthma to the medical exemption list.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government currently has no plans to review or amend the list of medical conditions that entitle someone to apply for a medical exemption certificate.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Civil Servants: Reserve Forces
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what steps the Government is taking to encourage civil servants to serve as reservists in the Armed Forces.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the Hon Member in the time available before Prorogation
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: After School Clubs: Primary Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps she is taking to ensure that primary schools offer a broad range of after school clubs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is committed to ensuring that all children and young people across England can access a variety of enrichment opportunities at school, including after school clubs, as part of our mission to break down barriers to opportunity. A new Enrichm...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education
